opts: Pass out to a teammate skating along the far perimeter side
Shoot quickly to the left where the goalie is out of position
Stickhandle and wait patiently for a much cleaner shooting angle
Turn away and skate the puck back out toward the blue line
Argue loudly with the referee nearby
Go pressure the puck carrier immediately
Glide back toward your bench for a quick change
Stand still and watch the play unfold up ice
Skate straight at them as fast as you can and try to knock the puck loose
Back up and maintain a steady gap while angling them toward the boards
Spin around and skate backward immediately all the way back to your crease
Stand your ground and keep both feet planted firmly on the ice
Force a pass to a covered winger and hope it works
Walk out from behind the net alone and try to carry it yourself
Pass across to the open defenseman to reset the breakout
Shoot the puck hard around the boards and skate off for a change
Shoot it down the ice to the far end as hard as you possibly can right off the boards
Skate it out calmly — the forecheckers are out of position and you have time
Wait behind your own net for the forecheckers to get tired and then regroup later
Pass it back to your goalie and let them freeze the puck for a clean faceoff
Take the shot yourself — you already have a clean lane open
Pass to your unmarked teammate cutting to the net
Dump the puck deep into the corner and chase it
Hold the puck and stickhandle to wait for a better play
Rush hard at the puck carrier to try to poke the puck away before the pass can be made
Take away the pass — stay in the middle, force the shooter, and let the goalie handle the shot
Chase the trailing player and leave the puck carrier to drive the net alone on your goalie there
Back up all the way to your own crease and let the goalie handle the two attackers alone
Skate deep into the zone with your two forwards and forecheck them
Stay at the top of the zone near the blue line as the safety
Head toward your bench quickly to line up for a change
Skate down to the half-wall spot and wait for a pass there
Reset and regroup calmly behind your own net before moving up ice
Go — skate it out fast and attack while they're out of position
Pass to your D and let them carry it up the ice and decide
Shoot it down the ice and chase it into their zone fast
Try to outskate the forechecker and carry it yourself up the wall fast
Quickly pass to the open defenseman before the forechecker arrives
Stop behind the net and wait until the forechecker backs off
Shoot the puck hard down the ice yourself for a full-zone clear
Shoot from where you are — any shot on net is a good shot from this angle for sure
Pass across to your teammate at the far post — the goalie can't get there in time
Skate the puck toward center ice and take a shot from a much better spot out front
Dump the puck behind the net and go fight for it in the corner with your teammate
Stop them cold by skating straight at them and poking the puck off their stick quickly
Angle your body to steer them along the boards and away from the middle of the ice
Back up all the way into your crease area and let the goalie handle the rush
Wait for them to shoot from a bad angle and then block the shot with your body fast
Force the pass to the covered strong-side winger and hope they win it
Pass to the open weak-side winger — they're the clean option
Try to skate it out of the zone yourself up the wall
Rim the puck around the boards to the far-side corner area
Charge full speed to try to meet them
Back up at a controlled pace
Spin and race back to your net
Stand still and wait it out
Stop the puck first and then look around for a better pass option
One-time it to the right side where the goalie hasn't moved yet
Stickhandle and wait patiently for a teammate to open up in the slot
Pass it back up to the point defenseman for a fresh look at net
Both defenders chase the puck carrier together to strip the puck off them before they can get a shot attempt
Take your side — one defender takes the carrier, the other takes the second attacker — and protect the middle
Both defenders collapse into the crease and pack the net tight to block every shot attempt they even try taking
Skate straight back to the goal line and let the goalie handle the 2-on-2 rush by themselves in the crease
Carry the puck in at full speed and try to blow right past the defender with pure speed
Chip the puck into the zone behind the defender and skate hard to get it first
Stop at the blue line and wait for your teammates to catch up to you before entering
Pass it back to your D and let the team regroup for a fresh entry attempt
Skate away toward center ice to get clear of the corner battle
Get close enough to be an easy pass — support behind or beside them
Call for a long stretch pass up past the blue line and break out fast
Stand still and watch the battle play out before you get involved
Pass to your teammate just as you planned from the start of the rush
Shoot — the goalie has moved and there's a lane on your side
Pass it back to the other side again for another look at the net
Pull up near the blue line and regroup with your team
Rush hard at the puck carrier to try to strip the puck before they get into your zone
Take your side and force the carrier wide — your partner protects the middle attacker
Chase the trailing attacker down the far side and leave the puck carrier to your partner
Back up all the way to your crease and let the goalie face the three attackers
Waiting with your stick flat on the ice, ready to catch and control the puck cleanly on contact
Looking up and deciding where you're going with the puck before it arrives — pre-reading the play
Calling loudly for the puck so your teammate knows you are ready and can deliver a solid tape-to-tape pass
Watching the puck travel the whole way to make sure that you don't miss it when it arrives
Chase the puck carrier with everyone on the ice and try to strip it back fast
One forward pressures the puck, the others recover their positions — don't panic
All five skaters retreat into your own zone and collapse tightly around the net area
Ignore the rush — you're still on the power play and will get another chance
Skate out of the way so the goalie can see the puck clearly and make a clean easy save
Stay planted and block the goalie's sightline — screen them, and be ready to tip the shot
Skate toward the corner early to get into rebound position off the boards after the shot is taken
Call loudly for a pass instead of the shot so you can score from a better angle out front
Stay with your original assignment and ignore the switch call from your partner completely
Take the attacker cutting to your side, let your partner recover their original man
Call the referee over for a quick penalty review on the play that just happened there
Back up all the way to the crease and try to cover both of the attackers at once
Wheel around the net and try to carry the puck all by yourself straight through the pressure up the middle
Reverse the puck to the other side — one forechecker can't follow, buying time for your winger to get position
Rim it hard around the boards and just hope your winger picks it up quickly before their D pinches down on them
Shoot it down the ice as far as you can for an icing call to get a whistle and a fresh faceoff in your end
Keep carrying the puck in at full speed on every single rush until the trap finally breaks down and lets you through cleanly
Pull the puck back to your defensemen, use a D-to-D pass to overload one side, or try a stretch pass to a breaking forward
Dump the puck deep every single rush and grind them down with a heavy forecheck and put lots of pressure on their D
Skate around the outside of the trap along the boards and curl back into the middle of the ice with tons of speed
Nothing at all — players should just react to where the puck goes after the official drops it clean on the ice
Their assignment — who they're covering and where they're going based on whether you win or lose the draw
Where the nearest door to the bench is in case their shift ends right after the puck drops on the ice
To skate as fast as they possibly can toward the puck the moment it hits the ice after the draw
Charge at them and try to knock the puck off their stick before they cross the red line
Back up at a pace that doesn't let them get speed on you, angle them toward the boards, and wait
Turn around and race them straight back to your net to try to beat them to the front of the crease
Stand completely still at the red line and wait for them to come to you before you make your move
